page: Microsoft Azure EC48eds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEDV5 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel processor, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 48 dedicated virtual CPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type includes 1441 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no integrated GPUs present on this instance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the EC48eds_v5 offers high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The combination of 384.0 GB of RAM and local storage makes this server well-suited for large databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the need for hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure EC48eds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEDV5 family, running on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high-density memory allocation of 8.0 GB per core. The instance is equipped with 1441 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for resource-intensive environments, this server provides a balanced combination of high memory capacity and dedicated compute resources. It is engineered for workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and enterprise-grade applications that demand substantial memory footprints and reliable local storage performance.
